Alliaria petiolata is a perennial herbaceous plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae), characterized by its white or pink flowers and pungent garlic-like odor.

The health benefits of Alliaria petiolata include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. It also has been traditionally used to treat digestive problems and skin conditions.

The bioactive constituents of Alliaria petiolata include isothiocyanates, such as allyl isothiocyanate, and other sulfur-containing compounds, which are responsible for its pungent smell and potential medicinal properties.

Alliaria petiolata is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to take advantage of its potential health benefits.
